title: spergula maxima subsp. linicola
intro: Spergula maxima subsp. linicola, also known as Corn Spurrey, is a perennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is often used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Spergula maxima subsp. linicola include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
